summary: Iteration 80 - Complete testing of Knowledge Graph Dashboard. All 15 features verified: 1) /knowledge-graph page loads successfully, 2) Stats cards show Graph Nodes (41), Relationships (52), Total AUM ($5.20M), Active Insights (3), Pending Actions (3), 3) Graph tab shows interactive force-directed visualization with nodes and links, 4) Filter dropdown filters graph by node type (Client, Portfolio, Asset, etc.), 5) Node click on canvas supported (clicks handled by react-force-graph-2d), 6) Actions tab shows 3 pending actions with 'Adjust & Execute' buttons, 7) Clicking 'Adjust & Execute' opens dialog with adjustable parameter sliders (Trade Amount $1K-$625K, Execution Timeframe 1-90 days, Price Limit 0-20%), 8) Execute Action button visible in dialog, 9) AI question input accepts natural language questions, 10) Ask button submits question and displays AI response, 11) Quick action badges set predefined questions, 12) Insights tab shows 4 active insights with severity badges (Severity 5 for Crypto Concentration Risk), 13) Risks tab shows retirement risks and cross-client risks, 14) Opportunities tab shows revenue opportunities with Total Potential per client, 15) All backend APIs return 200 OK with correct data structures.

minor.issue: AI response returns raw JSON data instead of natural language answer. The fallback handler returns data dump when LLM is not available or fails to generate proper response.

integration_issues.issue: AI answer displays raw JSON data (e.g., 'Based on the data: {"clients": [...]}') instead of natural language summary. This is a known minor formatting issue.

action_items: OPTIONAL: Improve AI engine fallback to format raw data into readable natural language instead of JSON dump
critical_code_review_comments: KnowledgeGraphDashboard.jsx correctly implements all required features: stats cards, interactive graph using react-force-graph-2d, tabbed interface (Graph, Overview, Insights, Actions, Risks, Opportunities)
routes.py has comprehensive API endpoints for graph overview, visualization, actions, insights, and AI Q&A
graph_db.py EmbeddedGraph class provides in-memory mock data with proper node/relationship structure (MOCKED - not connected to real Neo4j)
ai_engine.py has proper LLM integration with emergentintegrations but fallback returns raw JSON data
query_engine.py correctly calculates retirement risks, revenue opportunities, and cross-client risks
Adjustable action parameters (amount, timeframe, price_limit, min_yield, risk_adjustment, drift_tolerance, max_tax_impact) properly configured per action type
All interactive elements have data-testid attributes for testing
seed_data_creation: None - graph database uses MOCKED EmbeddedGraph class with demo financial data (6 clients, 3 portfolios, 9 assets, 4 insights, 4 actions)
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 80 verified Knowledge Graph Dashboard. All features working: force-directed graph visualization with react-force-graph-2d, filter dropdown, stats cards, 6 tabs (Graph, Overview, Insights, Actions, Risks, Opportunities). Adjust & Execute dialog has working sliders for action parameters. AI Q&A endpoint works but returns raw JSON (minor issue). Graph data is MOCKED via EmbeddedGraph class - not connected to real Neo4j database.

ai_question_input.placeholder: e.g., Which clients are most at risk for retirement?

ai_response.issue: Returns raw JSON instead of natural language
ai_response.note: Functional but formatting could be improved

mocked_apis.graph_database: MOCKED - EmbeddedGraph class in graph_db.py uses in-memory dictionaries instead of real Neo4j database
mocked_apis.financial_data: MOCKED - Demo data includes 6 clients (Wheeler, Smith, Johnson families), 3 portfolios, 9 assets (CBA, BHP, CSL, etc.)
mocked_apis.ai_fallback: LLM integration via emergentintegrations, falls back to raw JSON data if unavailable
